message:
fix(storage): correct flaky stale_lock_cleanup test by using non-zero max_age

The test `stale_lock_cleanup_alive_pid_unknown_start_ticks_not_removed` was
intermittently failing because it called `try_clean_stale_git_lock` with
`max_age_seconds = 0.0`. This made the age-based fallback threshold `0.0 * 2 = 0.0`,
so any lock file created even a millisecond ago would exceed the threshold and get
removed — defeating the test's purpose of verifying alive-PID protection.

Changed to `max_age_seconds = 3600.0` so the age-based fallback never triggers for
a freshly-created lock file.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
